extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. Transition [t4] sets sent:=true on send_attempt when (opted_out and acc==10), which can hold while opted_out is true. To exploit it, acc must reach 10. The step functions are step_0: acc:=(3*acc+15) mod 16, step_1 and step_2: acc:=(3*acc+5) mod 16 (the triple nested if-then-else implements mod-16 reduction; max input 3*15+15=60 needs at most 3 subtractions of 16, which the expression provides). Starting acc=0: step_0 -> 15, step_0 -> 12, step_1 -> 9, step_0 -> 10. Now acc=10. Then optout fires [t1]: opted_out:=true, sent:=false. Then send_attempt: since opted_out and acc==10, [t4] fires setting sent:=true while opted_out=true. State (opted_out=true, sent=true) violates opted_out implies not sent. The execution is 6 events (<=18). Simulated under the full transition semantics including default unchanged behavior; confirmed reachable.
